signals.headline: South Korea's annual inflation rate rose to 2
signals.raw: South Korea's annual inflation rate rose to 2.2% in March 2026 (from 2.0% in Feb), confirmed April 2 by Statistics Korea—below market consensus of 2.3-2.4% due to oil prices and govt curbs; BOK expected to hold rates at April meeting | NO** | MED confidence[1][2][3][4]
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: YoY CPI hit **2
signals.raw: YoY CPI hit **2.2%** (up from 2.0%), MoM +0.34% (vs Feb's 0.3%), driven by petroleum +9.9% YoY but offset by fuel caps/food vouchers; core inflation dipped to 2.2%[1][2][3].

signals.headline: BOK views risks rising post-April but holds at 2
signals.raw: BOK views risks rising post-April but holds at 2.5% now, per experts[2][3].

signals.headline: No events since April 2 (next data May 6) qualify as "right now" breaking news to shift prediction markets like BOK hike
signals.raw: No events since April 2 (next data May 6) qualify as "right now" breaking news to shift prediction markets like BOK hikes or targets[1][2][3][4].
